SNGI
SNGI
USB installer builder
1 System check
2 Builder setup
3 Recovery image
4 Build
5 Flash USB
6 Finished
Not affiliated with Valve or NVIDIA.

System check

This app turns Valve's official SteamOS recovery image into a bootable USB installer with the NVIDIA driver baked in — built locally in a private WSL2 Linux environment, nothing redistributed.

    
        
    
          
          
          
          
        
    
      
    

      
      
    
        
    Builder setup
    
        
    A private Arch Linux environment (steamos-builder) is created
        inside WSL2 to do the heavy lifting: compiling the NVIDIA kernel module against SteamOS's
        exact kernel. One-time, ~2 GB, fully removable later.
    
        
    
          
    
          
    
        
    
        
    Ready.
    
        
    
          
          
          
          
        
    
      
    

      
      
    
        
    SteamOS recovery image
    
        
    Valve requires you to download the recovery image yourself and accept
        their terms on the download page. Get it, then drop the file below —
        no need to extract it, the .img.bz2 is fine as-is.
    
        
    
          
        
    
        
    
          
    ⤓
    
          
    Drag the downloaded image here
    
            steamdeck-recovery-*.img.bz2 — or
    
          
        
    
        
    
        
    
          Advanced build options
          
          
          
          
          
        
    
        
    
          
          
        
    
      
    

      
      
    
        
    Build the installer image
    
        
    The image is copied into the builder, the current NVIDIA open driver is
        fetched from Arch Linux and compiled against SteamOS's kernel, and a one-click installer
        is added. Takes roughly 15–30 minutes.
    
        
    
          
    
          
    
        
    
        
    Ready to build.
    
        
    
          
          
          
          
        
    
      
    

      
      
    
        
    Flash to USB
    
        
    Plug in a USB stick of 16 GB or more.
    
        
    
        
    
          
        
    
        
    No USB drives found yet.
    
        
    ⚠ Everything on the selected drive will be permanently erased.
    
        
    
          
    
          
    
        
    
        
    
        
    
          
          
        
    
      
    

      
      
    
        
    USB installer ready 🎉
    
        
    Take the USB stick to the target machine:
    
        
      
          
    1. Enter the target machine's UEFI settings and disable Secure Boot.
      2. 
          
    2. Boot from the USB stick (UEFI boot menu, usually F8/F11/F12/Del).
      3. 
          
    3. It boots into a SteamOS desktop. Double-click
            “Install SteamOS (NVIDIA) to Hard Drive”
            (or Upgrade to keep games & data on an existing install).
      4. 
          
    4. Pick the target disk and confirm. The machine powers off when done.
      5. 
          
    5. Remove the USB stick and boot — first boot lands in Steam's setup.
      6. 
        
    
        
    Needs an NVIDIA RTX 20-series or newer GPU.
        If the first boot looks black for a while, wait — SteamOS hides its boot console.
